ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. FIREMAN'S FUND IS ALLOWED TO BRING SAID MOTION. (SEE WESTERN HERITAGE INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2011) 199 CAL.APP.4TH 1196.) THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA ON SEPTEMBER 21, 2012 AND THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA ON DECEMBER 8, 2015 ARE SET ASIDE PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S INHERENT, EQUITABLE POWER TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT ON THE GROUND OF EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE. RELIEF IS NOT AUTHORIZED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 473(B) BECAUSE THE MOTION WAS FILED MORE THAN SIX MONTHS AFTER BOTH ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THERE IS EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT, DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER SUCH EXPOSURE WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING OR CONTRIBUTING TO HIS RISK OF DEVELOPING HIS ASBESTOS-RELATED ILLNESS. ORDER ON MOTION AND PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS SIGNED AFTER THE HEARING. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: KYLE MCLEAN, CSR #13787 OF AIKEN WELCH

Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jay Wanless

RULING:
DEFENDANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 21, 2012 and the default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 8, 2015 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Relief is not authorized pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) because the motion was filed more than six months after both entry of default and default judgment. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Dana Freed of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10602 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Assuming without deciding that there is evidence creating a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related illness.
